title: memecylon stenophyllum
intro: Memecylon stenophyllum, also known as the East Indian cherry, is a plant species belonging to the family Melastomataceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The leaves and fruits of Memecylon stenophyllum are believed to have health benefits such as lowering blood sugar levels and aiding in the management of various health conditions like fever and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Memecylon stenophyllum are typically made by boiling the leaves and fruits in water to create a tea or infusion for medicinal use.
